chip.label: Onboard a new customer via REST + Slack
chip.prompt: When a customer signs up through our form, enrich their profile from the CRM, create their account, enroll them in the welcome campaign, and post a welcome message to Slack.

steps.name: Customer signs up
steps.narration: Placing a None Start Event bound to a public form — the process starts when the signup form is submitted.
steps.rationale:
Configure in the properties panel:
• Form → Type: Camunda Form (linked)
• Form → Form ID: customer-signup-form

Why: The prompt says the trigger is a form submission, not a message from another system. In Camunda 8 this is a None Start Event with a linked Camunda Form (zeebe:formDefinition formId=...). The form's field keys (email, firstName, lastName, company) become top-level process variables — you'll reference them as =email, =company, etc. in the next steps' Result Expression and request bodies.

substeps.why: The form's field keys (email, firstName, lastName, company) become top-level process variables consumed by later steps.

steps.name: Enrich profile from CRM
steps.narration: Adding a REST Outbound Connector task — GETs the customer's enrichment data so later steps have a richer profile to work with.
steps.rationale:
Configure in the properties panel:
• Template: REST Outbound Connector (applied automatically)
• HTTP Endpoint → Method: GET
• HTTP Endpoint → URL: ="https://api.crm.example.com/lookup?email=" + email
• Authentication → Type: Bearer token
• Authentication → Bearer token: {{secrets.CRM_API_TOKEN}}
• Output mapping → Result Expression: ={ enrichedProfile: response.body.profile, leadScore: response.body.score }

Why: REST calls use FEEL — prefix with = to evaluate. String concatenation uses +, and email comes from the form in the previous step. Secrets are injected via {{secrets.NAME}} and must stay inside the double quotes so Zeebe resolves them at runtime rather than at design time. Use Result Expression (not Result Variable) when you want named top-level variables — Result Variable would dump the entire response object under one key, forcing nested access downstream.

substeps.why: GET is idempotent and has no side effects — a safe default for a profile lookup.
REST calls use FEEL — prefix with = to evaluate. String concat with +, and email was emitted by the form in step 1.
The CRM expects an Authorization: Bearer header.
Secrets must be wrapped in {{secrets.NAME}} so Zeebe resolves them at runtime, not design time. Never paste raw tokens into the XML.
Use Result Expression (not Result Variable) to extract named top-level vars — Result Variable would dump the whole response under one key, forcing nested access downstream.

steps.name: Create customer account
steps.narration: Another REST call — POST to the CRM to actually create the account record.

substeps.why: POST creates a new resource — the CRM will allocate a customer ID and return it in the response body.
A static URL this time — no FEEL prefix needed because there's nothing to interpolate.
Same CRM, same secret — always reference via {{secrets.NAME}} so the token never lands in the BPMN XML.

substeps.value: ={ email: email, firstName: firstName, lastName: lastName, company: company, leadScore: leadScore, source: "onboarding-flow" }
substeps.why: POST bodies are FEEL maps — prefix with = and use { key: value } syntax. leadScore is in scope because step 2's Result Expression emitted it as a top-level variable.
Avoid naming outputs body, url, method, headers, or authentication — those are reserved for the connector's inputs and would clobber them on the next call.

steps.name: Enroll in welcome campaign
steps.narration: Third REST call — enrolls the new customer in the marketing welcome campaign.
steps.rationale:
Configure in the properties panel:
• HTTP Endpoint → Method: POST
• HTTP Endpoint → URL: ="https://api.marketing.example.com/campaigns/welcome/enroll"
• Payload → Body: ={ customerId: customerId, email: email }
• Authentication → API key header → Name: X-API-Key, Value: {{secrets.MARKETING_API_KEY}}
• Task definition → Retries: 5

Why: Third-party marketing APIs have transient failures — bumping retries from the default 3 to 5 lets Zeebe auto-retry on 5xx / network errors without raising an incident. The task only fails (incident) after retries are exhausted, at which point the operator can inspect and resume. The customerId used here was produced by step 3's Result Expression — the previous-step-output chain is how Camunda connectors stay stateless without you passing anything explicitly.

substeps.why: Enrollment creates a subscription record on the marketing side — POST is the right verb.
Static endpoint — the campaign ID is embedded in the path, so no interpolation needed.
The marketing platform authenticates via a custom header, not Bearer — the header name is fixed by their API.
Different vendor, different secret — keep marketing credentials separate from CRM credentials so rotation is independent.
Third-party marketing APIs have transient failures — bumping retries from the default 3 to 5 lets Zeebe auto-retry on 5xx / network errors before raising an incident.

steps.name: Post welcome to Slack
steps.narration: Slack Outbound Connector — posts the welcome message to the customer-success channel.
steps.rationale:
Configure in the properties panel:
• Template: Slack Outbound Connector
• Authentication → OAuth token: {{secrets.SLACK_BOT_TOKEN}}
• Method: Post Message
• Channel/User name: #customer-success
• Message type: Plain text
• Message: ="👋 New customer onboarded: " + firstName + " " + lastName + " (" + company + ") — lead score " + string(leadScore)

Why: Slack requires a bot token with the chat:write scope — OAuth token must be wrapped in {{secrets.NAME}} so it's never committed to the BPMN XML. Channel names start with # (or @username for DMs). Use Plain text for simple strings; Message block is the switch you'd flip if you wanted Block Kit JSON. Note the string(leadScore) cast — FEEL's + is both numeric addition and string concatenation, and it throws a type error if you try to concatenate a number directly to a string.

substeps.why: Slack requires a bot token with the chat:write scope. Wrap in {{secrets.NAME}} so the raw token never hits the BPMN XML.
Post Message maps to Slack's chat.postMessage API — the standard way to deliver a one-shot notification to a channel or DM.
Channel names start with # — use @username for DMs instead. The bot must be invited to private channels before it can post.
Plain text for simple strings; Message block is the switch you'd flip if you wanted Block Kit JSON.

substeps.value: ="👋 New customer onboarded: " + firstName + " " + lastName + " (" + company + ") — lead score " + string(leadScore)
substeps.why: Note the string(leadScore) cast — FEEL's + is both numeric addition and string concatenation, and it throws a type error if you concatenate a number directly to a string.

steps.name: Customer onboarded
steps.narration: End Event — the process completes successfully. Nothing to configure for a None End Event; if you later wanted to publish a 'customer.onboarded' event for downstream processes to react to, you'd change the type to Message End Event here and leave the rest of the diagram alone.
steps.rationale:
Configure in the properties panel: nothing required for a None End Event.

Why: Every BPMN path must terminate. A None End Event marks successful completion with no side effects — no message published, no error thrown. If you later wanted to publish a 'customer.onboarded' event for downstream processes to react to, you'd change the type to Message End Event here and leave the rest of the diagram alone.

resultXml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<bpmn:definitions xmlns:bpmn="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL" xmlns:bpmndi="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/DI" xmlns:dc="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DC" xmlns:di="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DI" xmlns:zeebe="http://camunda.org/schema/zeebe/1.0" xmlns:modeler="http://camunda.org/schema/modeler/1.0" id="Definitions_customer_onboarding" targetNamespace="http://bpmn.io/schema/bpmn" exporter="Camunda Modeler" exporterVersion="5.0.0" modeler:executionPlatform="Camunda Cloud" modeler:executionPlatformVersion="8.5.0">
  <bpmn:process id="Process_customer_onboarding" isExecutable="true">
    <bpmn:startEvent id="StartEvent_signup" name="Customer signs up">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>
        <zeebe:formDefinition formId="customer-signup-form" />
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_signup_to_fetch</bpmn:outgoing>
    </bpmn:startEvent>
    <bpmn:serviceTask id="Task_fetchProfile" name="Enrich profile from CRM" zeebe:modelerTemplate="io.camunda.prototype.HttpJson">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>
        <zeebe:taskDefinition type="io.camunda:http-json:1" retries="3" />
        <zeebe:ioMapping>
          <zeebe:input source="GET" target="method" />
          <zeebe:input source="="https://api.crm.example.com/lookup?email=" + email" target="url" />
          <zeebe:input source="bearer" target="authentication.type" />
          <zeebe:input source="{{secrets.CRM_API_TOKEN}}" target="authentication.token" />
        </zeebe:ioMapping>
        <zeebe:taskHeaders>
          <zeebe:header key="resultExpression" value="={ enrichedProfile: response.body.profile, leadScore: response.body.score }" />
        </zeebe:taskHeaders>
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_signup_to_fetch</bpmn:incoming>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_fetch_to_create</bpmn:outgoing>
    </bpmn:serviceTask>
    <bpmn:serviceTask id="Task_createCustomer" name="Create customer account" zeebe:modelerTemplate="io.camunda.prototype.HttpJson">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>
        <zeebe:taskDefinition type="io.camunda:http-json:1" retries="3" />
        <zeebe:ioMapping>
          <zeebe:input source="POST" target="method" />
          <zeebe:input source="https://api.crm.example.com/customers" target="url" />
          <zeebe:input source="bearer" target="authentication.type" />
          <zeebe:input source="{{secrets.CRM_API_TOKEN}}" target="authentication.token" />
          <zeebe:input source="={ email: email, firstName: firstName, lastName: lastName, company: company, leadScore: leadScore, source: "onboarding-flow" }" target="body" />
        </zeebe:ioMapping>
        <zeebe:taskHeaders>
          <zeebe:header key="resultExpression" value="={ customerId: response.body.id }" />
        </zeebe:taskHeaders>
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_fetch_to_create</bpmn:incoming>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_create_to_enroll</bpmn:outgoing>
    </bpmn:serviceTask>
    <bpmn:serviceTask id="Task_enrollCampaign" name="Enroll in welcome campaign" zeebe:modelerTemplate="io.camunda.prototype.HttpJson">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>
        <zeebe:taskDefinition type="io.camunda:http-json:1" retries="5" />
        <zeebe:ioMapping>
          <zeebe:input source="POST" target="method" />
          <zeebe:input source="https://api.marketing.example.com/campaigns/welcome/enroll" target="url" />
          <zeebe:input source="apiKey" target="authentication.type" />
          <zeebe:input source="X-API-Key" target="authentication.apiKeyName" />
          <zeebe:input source="{{secrets.MARKETING_API_KEY}}" target="authentication.apiKeyValue" />
          <zeebe:input source="={ customerId: customerId, email: email }" target="body" />
        </zeebe:ioMapping>
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_create_to_enroll</bpmn:incoming>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_enroll_to_slack</bpmn:outgoing>
    </bpmn:serviceTask>
    <bpmn:serviceTask id="Task_slackNotify" name="Post welcome to Slack" zeebe:modelerTemplate="io.camunda.prototype.Slack">
      <bpmn:extensionElements>
        <zeebe:taskDefinition type="io.camunda:slack:1" retries="3" />
        <zeebe:ioMapping>
          <zeebe:input source="{{secrets.SLACK_BOT_TOKEN}}" target="token" />
          <zeebe:input source="chat.postMessage" target="method" />
          <zeebe:input source="#customer-success" target="data.channel" />
          <zeebe:input source="plainText" target="data.messageType" />
          <zeebe:input source="="👋 New customer onboarded: " + firstName + " " + lastName + " (" + company + ") — lead score " + string(leadScore)" target="data.text" />
        </zeebe:ioMapping>
      </bpmn:extensionElements>
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_enroll_to_slack</bpmn:incoming>
      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_slack_to_end</bpmn:outgoing>
    </bpmn:serviceTask>
    <bpmn:endEvent id="EndEvent_onboarded" name="Customer onboarded">
      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_slack_to_end</bpmn:incoming>
    </bpmn:endEvent>
    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_signup_to_fetch" sourceRef="StartEvent_signup" targetRef="Task_fetchProfile" />
    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_fetch_to_create" sourceRef="Task_fetchProfile" targetRef="Task_createCustomer" />
    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_create_to_enroll" sourceRef="Task_createCustomer" targetRef="Task_enrollCampaign" />
    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_enroll_to_slack" sourceRef="Task_enrollCampaign" targetRef="Task_slackNotify" />
    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_slack_to_end" sourceRef="Task_slackNotify" targetRef="EndEvent_onboarded" />
  </bpmn:process>
  <bpmndi:BPMNDiagram id="BPMNDiagram_1">
    <bpmndi:BPMNPlane id="BPMNPlane_1" bpmnElement="Process_customer_onboarding">
      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="StartEvent_signup_di" bpmnElement="StartEvent_signup">
        <dc:Bounds x="180" y="182" width="36" height="36" />
        <bpmndi:BPMNLabel>
          <dc:Bounds x="156" y="225" width="86" height="14" />
        </bpmndi:BPMNLabel>
      </bpmndi:BPMNShape>
      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="Task_fetchProfile_di" bpmnElement="Task_fetchProfile">
        <dc:Bounds x="270" y="160" width="100" height="80" />
      </bpmndi:BPMNShape>
      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="Task_createCustomer_di" bpmnElement="Task_createCustomer">
        <dc:Bounds x="430" y="160" width="100" height="80" />
      </bpmndi:BPMNShape>
      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="Task_enrollCampaign_di" bpmnElement="Task_enrollCampaign">
        <dc:Bounds x="590" y="160" width="100" height="80" />
      </bpmndi:BPMNShape>
      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="Task_slackNotify_di" bpmnElement="Task_slackNotify">
        <dc:Bounds x="750" y="160" width="100" height="80" />
      </bpmndi:BPMNShape>
      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="EndEvent_onboarded_di" bpmnElement="EndEvent_onboarded">
        <dc:Bounds x="912" y="182" width="36" height="36" />
        <bpmndi:BPMNLabel>
          <dc:Bounds x="889" y="225" width="83" height="14" />
        </bpmndi:BPMNLabel>
      </bpmndi:BPMNShape>
      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="Flow_signup_to_fetch_di" bpmnElement="Flow_signup_to_fetch">
        <di:waypoint x="216" y="200" />
        <di:waypoint x="270" y="200" />
      </bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="Flow_fetch_to_create_di" bpmnElement="Flow_fetch_to_create">
        <di:waypoint x="370" y="200" />
        <di:waypoint x="430" y="200" />
      </bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="Flow_create_to_enroll_di" bpmnElement="Flow_create_to_enroll">
        <di:waypoint x="530" y="200" />
        <di:waypoint x="590" y="200" />
      </bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="Flow_enroll_to_slack_di" bpmnElement="Flow_enroll_to_slack">
        <di:waypoint x="690" y="200" />
        <di:waypoint x="750" y="200" />
      </bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="Flow_slack_to_end_di" bpmnElement="Flow_slack_to_end">
        <di:waypoint x="850" y="200" />
        <di:waypoint x="912" y="200" />
      </bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
    </bpmndi:BPMNPlane>
  </bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>
</bpmn:definitions>
